stories.headline: Hegseth says clock paused on deadline to seek approval for Iran war
stories.summary: The US defence secretary says the timer for the president to report to Congress "pauses or stops" in a ceasefire.

stories.headline: A deadline for the Iran war is here. What does the War Powers Act say?
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asserted an ongoing ceasefire “pauses” the clock on the 60-day deadline to end hostilities in Iran or seek congressional approval.

stories.headline: Has the US-Iran ceasefire reset the clock on War Powers Act deadline?
stories.summary: Defence secretary claims hostilities have ceased, but lawmakers say US forces remain active despite halted air raids.

stories.headline: Fertiliser crisis caused by Iran war sparks global food security fears
stories.summary: The Iran war and the consequent blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has triggered a severe "fertiliser squeeze" as soaring energy costs and export disruptions push farmers' affordability to a four-year low.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: Young Palestinian artists in Gaza showcase war experiences through outdoor exhibition
stories.summary: Young Palestinian artists in Gaza have staged an impromptu open-air exhibition, turning war-damaged walls into a gallery. For many, the works are deeply personal and were created after fleeing their homes.

stories.headline: Iran war may cause food shortages in Africa, world’s largest fertiliser firm says
stories.summary: Yara CEO warns of global auction that would leave poorest countries scrambling for supplies they can ill affordThe Iran war could have “dramatic consequences”, causing food shortages and price rises in some of Africa’s poorest and most vulnerable communities, the head of the world’s largest fertiliser company has said.Svein Tore Holsether, the chief executive of Yara International, said world leaders needed to guard against soaring prices and sho

stories.headline: Police say they will charge people with unrest in Alice Springs after arrest over death of Kumanjayi Little Baby
stories.summary: Five-year-old’s grandfather and senior Warlpiri elder appeals for calm, saying: ‘It is time now for sorry business’• Warning: This article contains references to and images of Indigenous Australians who have diedNorthern Territory police say one person is facing charges and more are expected to be over unrest in Alice Springs after the arrest of a man in connection with the death of five-year-old girl.The grandfather of Kumanjayi Little Baby, who

stories.headline: Oil prices rise again with little sign of war on Iran ending
stories.summary: Brent crude futures hit $126.41 on Thursday - a 5 percent rise for the week.

stories.headline: Iran war: What’s happening on day 63 as Trump signals possible attacks
stories.summary: Iran’s president calls the US siege 'intolerable' as Donald Trump says war may resume.

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

stories.headline: Deadly Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Seventeen people - including two children - were killed, according to Lebanon's health ministry.

stories.headline: What to Know About the Maine Senate Race After Janet Mills Drops Out
stories.summary: The exit of Gov. Janet Mills kicked off the general election early in what is likely to be one of the most important, expensive and combative Senate races of 2026.

stories.headline: ‘Profoundly distressing’: record number of deaths in custody and police operations in NSW
stories.summary: There were 66 deaths in custody and police operations in 2025 – 18 more than the previous yearWarning: This article contains references to Indigenous Australians who have diedThere were a record number of New South Wales deaths in custody and police operations last year, with almost a quarter of prisoner deaths recorded as self-harm, a coroner’s court report states.Of those who killed themselves, most were by hanging, despite millions being inves

stories.headline: Turkish police fire tear gas and arrest almost 400 people at May Day rallies
stories.summary: Earlier this week, authorities issued arrest warrants for 62 people, of whom they deemed 46, including journalists, trade unionists and opposition figures, were "likely to carry out attacks."

stories.headline: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Myanmar democracy icon detained for years
stories.summary: Little has been heard of Myanmar's former leader since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Exxon and Chevron defy Trump pressure to boost oil production
stories.summary: US supermajors stick to prewar strategies despite White House plea for more drilling to curb soaring petrol prices

stories.headline: Mills Exit Is a Blow to Schumer as Democrats Question His Strategy
stories.summary: Senator Chuck Schumer, the New York Democrat and minority leader, considered the Maine governor his top recruit for winning the majority. Critics said her collapse showed he is out of touch with the party’s voters.

stories.headline: The KOSPI's miracle surge comes with a warning label
UAE’s OPEC exit signals strategic shift as Gulf unity faces new test over oil policy
stories.summary: The UAE's exit from OPEC signals a strategic shift, raising questions about Gulf coordination and the organisation's future.

stories.headline: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.headline: My latest portfolio revealed
stories.summary: Now the Bank of England has called the top, I’ve never been so bullish about stocks

stories.headline: Apple credits ‘most popular’ ever iPhone for booming sales
stories.summary: John Ternus pledges to continue ‘deliberateness and discipline’ in first comments since being named next CEO

stories.headline: Shares in Japanese toilet maker Toto soar on AI-related pivot
stories.summary: Investors cheer after company unveils plans to boost output of semiconductor components

stories.headline: Emerging Assets Extend Gains as Iran Delivers New Proposal to US
stories.summary: Emerging-market benchmarks for stocks and currencies extended climb in holiday-thinned trade as Iran delivered a new proposal to the US via Pakistan while the Strait of Hormuz remains shut.

stories.headline: Moderna CEO on Earnings, Flu Shots and Skin Cancer Trials
stories.summary: Moderna’s first-quarter sales beat expectations, as the struggling vaccine maker that’s faced resistance from the Trump administration has found new growth outside the US. Moderna CEO Stephane Bancel sits down with Bloomberg's Katie Greifeld, saying the company's skin cancer trial results should arrive this year. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Futures Extend Gains on Lower Production, LNG Dip
stories.summary: US natural gas futures rose, adding to Thursday’s 12-cent jump, as producers rein in output and feedgas to the Cameron LNG terminal in Louisiana remains lower.

stories.headline: Billionaire’s Family Office Plots More Private Equity Exits
stories.summary: The billionaire UK founder of a home-repair business acquired by Brookfield Asset Management is working to ramp up his family office’s private equity exits, as larger buyout firms grapple with a dry spell for transactions.

stories.headline: Yen Traders on Alert After Japan’s $34.5 Billion Intervention
stories.summary: Yen traders are on watch for signs of further action by Japanese authorities after intervention triggered the currency’s sharpest rally in three years on Thursday.

stories.headline: Trump Vows Iran Pressure With Naval Blockade, Yen Resumes Rally | The Opening Trade 5/1/2026
stories.summary:
President Trump vowed to maintain a naval blockade on Iran and was briefed by commanders on further military options. 

The yen resumed its advance in late trading in Tokyo after an earlier pause in the rally triggered by Japan’s first intervention in the currency market since 2024. 

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of th

stories.headline: Trump Vows to Maintain Pressure on Iran Through Naval Blockade | The Pulse 5/1
stories.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Jari Stehn, Goldman Sachs, Chief European Economist; Phillips O’Brien, University of St Andrews, Strategic Studies Professor; Timothy Richards, Vue Founder and CEO.
 (S

stories.headline: Uganda copying Russia and China with new bill designed to crush dissent, say critics
stories.summary: New law proposes up to 20 years in prison for promoting ‘foreign interests’, and restricts those who work with or are funded by overseas partnersUgandan opposition figures, human rights organisations and legal experts have condemned a sweeping bill that proposes up to 20 years in prison for promoting “foreign interests”, and imposes restrictions on a broad range of people and organisations that work with or receive funding from overseas partners.

stories.headline: New Banksy sculpture appears to show politician blinded by his own flag
stories.summary: Banksy has unveiled a new sculpture of a man stepping off a stone base with his face obscured by a flag.

stories.headline: Scandal-hit 'baijiu' maker sharply revises earnings with chairman missing
Japan's Sumitomo Corp. to sell its Madagascar nickel mine business
Bangladesh clothing makers look to trade deals to revive exports
Contentious EU-Mercosur trade deal takes provisional effect
stories.summary: The EU-Mercosur free-trade deal took provisional effect on Friday despite a legal challenge before the Court of Justice of the European Union.

stories.headline: Workers' health isn't the only thing at risk of job strain — the economy is too, study finds
stories.summary: As Europe marks Labour Day, work strain is taking a deadly toll on employees worldwide. From burnout to anxiety, many Europeans say they dare not speak out about their concerns for fear of negative consequences.

stories.headline: Podcast | Is the future looking bright or bleak for European workers?
stories.summary: Can the EU become more competitive while preserving its workers’ rights? What role do trade unions have in a time of deregulation? Is AI going to replace Europeans’ jobs? Brussels, My Love? tries to answer these questions.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Voting Rights Ruling Could Fuel New Era of Redistricting Wars
stories.summary: The expected flood of new congressional maps is likely to produce fewer competitive districts, fewer ways for voters to hold elected officials accountable and more polarized politics.

stories.headline: Hegseth Says Cease-Fire Stops Clock on Deadline to Seek Approval for Iran War
stories.summary: The defense secretary testified on the eve of the 60-day mark of the war, a major statutory deadline for the president to withdraw forces or seek approval from Congress to continue the fight.

stories.headline: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval
stories.summary: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that expires Friday.

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.headline: Trump, Iran are locked in high-stakes standoff as oil prices hit 4-year high
stories.summary: As the U.S. blockade and Iranian mines and drones halt shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. gas prices also reached a level not seen since just after Russia’s Ukraine invasion.

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition
stories.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: How Conservatives on the Supreme Court Weakened the Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The Supreme Court just overturned Louisiana’s congressional voting map, landing the latest blow to the landmark Voting Rights Act. Abbie VanSickle, a reporter covering the court for The New York Times, explains.

stories.headline: Maine Gov. Janet Mills drops out of race to unseat Republican Sen. Susan Collins
stories.summary: The seat is critical to Democrats’ hopes of retaking the Senate. Graham Platner, an oyster farmer and Democrat, is likely to win the primary.

stories.headline: Brazil’s congress approves bill reducing prison sentence of former president Jair Bolsonaro
stories.summary: President Lula’s veto of the bill was overturned by Brazil’s congress and senate, meaning it now awaits confirmation by supreme court Brazil’s largely conservative congress has approved a bill reducing the prison sentence of the far-right former president Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted last year of attempting a coup.The bill had initially been passed by congress in December, but President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva vetoed it in January in a sym

stories.headline: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
stories.summary: Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

stories.headline: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.headline: Ukraine expands oil strikes on Russia as Putin proposes brief ceasefire
stories.summary: In a phone call with President Trump, Putin proposed a ceasefire on 9 May, the anniversary of the defeat of Nazi Germany in WW2.

stories.headline: Wada investigation finds 300 Russian doping cases
stories.summary: More than 300 sanctions have been handed to Russian athletes following the seizure of Moscow laboratory data in 2019, the World Anti-Doping Agency says.

stories.headline: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
stories.summary: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

stories.headline: S&P 500 Is Set for Longest Weekly Rally Since 2024: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A record-breaking run drove stocks toward their longest weekly rally since 2024 after a batch of solid corporate earnings, signs of economic strength and hopes for a deal to end the Iran war.

stories.headline: Senators ban themselves from participating in prediction markets
stories.summary: The platforms, which have gained scrutiny recently, allow people to place bets on future events such as election results. The ban also applies to Senate staffers.

stories.headline: 'How are we going to get back home?' Islamist group tightens blockade on Mali capital
stories.summary: The blockade comes days after Mali's defence minister was assassinated in a series of nationwide attacks.

stories.headline: China to ban drone sales in Beijing citing security concerns
stories.summary: Drone users will also be required to register their devices and secure permits before flying them.

stories.headline: Why is China banning drone sales in Beijing?
stories.summary: The BBC's Laura Bicker looks into why China has tightened regulations on drones, with authorities citing public safety concerns.

stories.headline: Gold Declines as Trump Vows to Maintain Pressure on Iran
stories.summary: Gold edged lower as traders weighed a standoff between the US and Iran that’s dashed expectations for rate cuts from central banks across the world.

stories.headline: Supreme Court limits key provision of the landmark Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: The decision could touch off a scramble by Republicans to redraw majority-minority congressional districts, especially in the South, that could cost many Black Democrats their seats.

stories.headline: What the Supreme Court did to the Voting Rights Act
stories.summary: Southern lawmakers can now draw congressional maps to break up minority voters, as the conservative majority continues to roll back decades-old civil rights protections.

stories.headline: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
stories.summary: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: Swashbuckling oil-services firms are preparing for a boom
stories.summary: Post-war reconstruction and efforts to diversify production will present big opportunities

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: Watch: Elderly woman rescued by robot from Ukraine's frontline
stories.summary: The Ukrainian army spotted the woman using a drone and sent the machine to her rescue as she was trying to leave her war-torn village.

stories.headline: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries has been extended by three weeks after talks in Washington.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: The Mexican government says the country is safe to visit for the World Cup.
